1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## Search Protocol
73	
74	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
75	
76	1. `lean_local_search` first
77	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
78	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
79	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
80	
81	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
82	
83	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
84	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
85	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
86	
87	## Tooling Traps & Verification
88	
89	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
90	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
91	
92	## Logging
93	
94	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
95	
96	**Format:**
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
100	
101	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
102	### Attempt 1
103	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
104	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
105	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
106	
107	### Attempt 2
108	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
109	- **Result:** RESOLVED
110	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
111	
112	## helper_bijective (line 78)
113	### Attempt 1
114	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
115	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
116	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
117	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
118	```
119	
120	**Rules:**
121	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
122	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
123	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
124	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
125	
126	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
127	
128	## Summary Pipeline
129	
130	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
131	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
132	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
133	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
134	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
135	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
136	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
137	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
138	
139	## End-of-session handoff
140	
141	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
142	
143	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
144	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
145	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
146	   - Concrete next step for the next session
147	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
148	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
149

47	## Iter-094 results — independently re-verified iter-095 plan pass

456	theorem cechCofaceMap_pi_smul
457	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
458	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
459	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) :
460	    let R := Γ(C.left, U)
461	    let K₀ := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
462	      (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))
463	    let scK₀ := HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n
464	    let Z₁ := fun (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀) =>
465	      ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj
466	        (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
467	    let Z₂ := fun (i : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀) =>
468	      ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj
469	        (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
470	    let e₁ := (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv
471	    let e₂ := (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).toLinearEquiv
472	    letI perI₁ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₁ i) := fun i => by
473	      apply RingHom.toModule
474	      refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
475	      let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
476	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
477	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
478	      have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
479	        basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
480	      exact h1.trans h2
481	    letI h_mod_pi₁ : Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
482	    letI perI₂ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₂ i) := fun i => by
483	      apply RingHom.toModule
484	      refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
485	      let a0 : Fin (n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
486	      have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
487	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
488	      have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
489	        basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
490	      exact h1.trans h2
491	    letI h_mod_pi₂ : Module R (∀ i, Z₂ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
492	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
493	      e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
494	        r • e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm y)) := by
495	  intro R K₀ scK₀ Z₁ Z₂ e₁ e₂ r y
496	  -- Reconstruct the letI block inside the body so subsequent tactics that
497	  -- reference perI₁/h_mod_pi₁/perI₂/h_mod_pi₂ by name find them in scope.
498	  -- The values are definitionally equal to those baked into the goal's `•`
499	  -- via instance synthesis (same Pi.module / RingHom.toModule construction).
500	  letI perI₁ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₁ i) := fun i => by
501	    apply RingHom.toModule
502	    refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
503	    let a0 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
504	    have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
505	      basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
506	    have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
507	      basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
508	    exact h1.trans h2
509	  letI h_mod_pi₁ : Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
510	  letI perI₂ : ∀ i, Module R (Z₂ i) := fun i => by
511	    apply RingHom.toModule
512	    refine (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ?_).op).hom
513	    let a0 : Fin (n + 1) := ⟨0, by omega⟩
514	    have h1 : ∏ᶜ (fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a)) ≤
515	      basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) := (Pi.π _ a0).le
516	    have h2 : basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a0) ≤ U :=
517	      basicOpen_le C.left (i a0 : Γ(C.left, U))
518	    exact h1.trans h2
519	  letI h_mod_pi₂ : Module R (∀ i, Z₂ i) := Pi.module _ _ _
520	  funext j
521	  simp only [Pi.smul_apply]
522	  have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n := by
523	    rcases n with _ | k
524	    · simp at hn
525	    · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
526	  dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor,
527	    toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]
528	  simp [FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor,
529	    FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor,
530	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex,
531	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj,
532	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
533	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.map,
534	    CochainComplex.of, FormalCoproduct.evalOp,
535	    CosimplicialObject.δ, e₁, e₂, ModuleCat.piIsoPi, dif_pos hRel]
536	  rw [show (limit.isoLimitCone
537	        ⟨ModuleCat.productCone Z₂, ModuleCat.productConeIsLimit Z₂⟩)
538	        = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂ from rfl,
539	      show (limit.isoLimitCone
540	        ⟨ModuleCat.productCone Z₁, ModuleCat.productConeIsLimit Z₁⟩)
541	        = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁ from rfl]
542	  show (ConcreteCategory.hom (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).hom) _ j =
543	      r • (ConcreteCategory.hom (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).hom) _ j
544	  rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply,
545	      ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply]
546	  -- S6 step (a) [iter-092 CORRECTION]: `ModuleCat.hom_sum` IS in Mathlib
547	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic` L359); earlier iterations
548	  -- (086+) misreported it as absent. We bind it as a local `have` with
549	  -- explicit ring instance (`R := k`) so the AddCommMonoid (M ⟶ N) synthesis
550	  -- succeeds during application (HOU fragility otherwise).
551	  have hom_sum_dist :
552	      ∀ {M N : ModuleCat.{u} k} {ι : Type}
553	        (f : ι → (M ⟶ N)) (s : Finset ι),
554	        ((∑ i ∈ s, f i) : M ⟶ N).hom = ∑ i ∈ s, (f i).hom :=
555	    @fun M N ι => ModuleCat.hom_sum (M := M) (N := N) (R := k)
556	  -- S6 step (b) [iter-093]: per-application form of `ModuleCat.hom_sum`.
557	  have key₁ :
558	      ∀ (F : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)))
559	        (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)),
560	        ((∑ i, F i) : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)).hom z =
561	          ∑ i, (F i).hom z := by
562	    intro F z
563	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum F Finset.univ]
564	    exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (F i).hom) z
565	  -- S6 step (b') [iter-094 BREAKTHROUGH]: fuse the eqToHom-wrapped ∘ₗ into a
566	  -- categorical `≫` composition. `ModuleCat.hom_comp f g : (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom`,
567	  -- so `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` converts `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom)` into
568	  -- `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom`. This sidesteps the def-equal-not-syntactic-equal
569	  -- ModuleCat mismatch that defeated iter-093's `LinearMap.comp_apply` attempts.
570	  rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]
571	  -- S6 step (c) [iter-094 partial]: body-local helper combining
572	  -- `Preadditive.sum_comp` + `ModuleCat.hom_sum` + `LinearMap.sum_apply`
573	  -- into a single per-application distribution. Proved with FREE `G`/`E`/`z`
574	  -- (not bound to the goal's summand) so the proof itself avoids HOU. The
575	  -- BLOCKER remaining is that `rw [key₂]` / `simp_rw [key₂]` on the post-(b')
576	  -- goal still HOU-fails: the summand `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun i_1 ↦ ... uses
577	  -- outer i ...)` has shadowing `i` binders that Lean's HOU pattern matcher
578	  -- cannot resolve when trying to instantiate the `G` placeholder. See
579	  -- task_results/Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.lean.md for iter-094 attempt log.
580	  have key₂ :
581	      ∀ (G : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)))
582	        (E : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ ModuleCat.of k ((i : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀) → ↑(Z₂ i)))
583	        (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)),
584	        ((∑ i, G i) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z := by
585	    intro G E z
586	    rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
587	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum (fun i => G i ≫ E) Finset.univ]
588	    exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (G i ≫ E).hom) z
589	  sorry
590	
591	-- Iter-078: lifted to 800000 because the iter-078 prover work on
592	-- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (per-summand R-linearity of the Čech differential)
593	-- requires further tactic steps inside the deeply-nested `have` block;
594	-- each additional rewrite touching the `(r • _)` smul triggers
595	-- `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose `whnf` cost exceeds the default
596	-- 200000 budget at the theorem head.
597	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
598	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
599	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
600	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
601	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
602	schemes in our setting.
603	
604	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
605	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
606	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
607	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
608	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
609	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
610	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
611	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
612	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
613	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
614	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
615	
616	The structural skeleton is:
617	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
618	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
619	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
620	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
621	
622	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
623	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
624	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
625	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
626	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
627	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
628	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
629	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
630	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
631	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
632	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
633	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
634	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
635	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
636	
637	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
638	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
639	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
640	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
641	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
642	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
643	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
644	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
645	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
646	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
647	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
648	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
649	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_

1	# Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean
2	
3	## `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` body L589 trailing sorry (iter-094)
4	
5	### Iter-094 outcome summary
6	
7	**Status**: PARTIAL substantive progress. File compiles, 6 sorries (unchanged
8	from iter-093). Genuine breakthrough on the eqToHom-blocker, plus proved
9	body-local infrastructure (`key₂`) for the next step. Sorry budget hard cap
10	(6) maintained.
11	
12	### Step (b) / Route (D-variant) — BREAKTHROUGH
13	
14	**Committed line: `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` (L583).**
15	
16	The iter-094 plan's Routes (D)/(E)/(F) were all aimed at sidestepping the
17	iter-093 blocker: `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom) z` couldn't be unfolded by
18	`LinearMap.comp_apply` due to def-equal-but-syntactically-distinct ModuleCat
19	mismatch (`∏ᶜ Z₂` vs Pi-product).
20	
21	**Discovery**: Mathlib's `ModuleCat.hom_comp f g : (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom`
22	provides a `rfl`-lemma whose **reverse direction** (`← ModuleCat.hom_comp`)
23	converts `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` into `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` — a categorical
24	composition. This sidesteps the LinearMap-level eqToHom blocker entirely.
25	
26	Tactic `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` succeeds with `lean_diagnostic_messages`
27	returning `[]` (severity=error) at the post-rewrite position.
28	
29	Post-(b') goal shape:
30	```
31	(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((∑ i, F i) ≫ eqToHom ⋯).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
32	  r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((∑ i, F i) ≫ eqToHom ⋯).hom (e₁.symm y))
33	```
34	where the eqToHom is now inside a categorical `≫`, not wrapping a `∘ₗ`.
35	
36	### Step (c) attempt — `key₂` infrastructure landed; HOU rewrite blocked
37	
38	**Committed**: body-local `have key₂` at L590–L599 (fully proved):
39	```lean
40	have key₂ : ∀ G E z, ((∑ i, G i) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z
41	```
42	The proof chains `Preadditive.sum_comp` → `ModuleCat.hom_sum` → `LinearMap.sum_apply`.
43	Proof succeeds because `G`, `E`, `z` are all FREE variables (no HOU).
44	
45	**Blocker**: `rw [key₂]` on the goal at L600 fails with
46	"pattern `(ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((∑ i, ?G i) ≫ ?E)) ?z` not found".
47	
48	The failure is HOU: the summand body
49	`(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ... ((SimplexCategory.δ i) ...) ...`
50	references the outer summation binder `i` in nested positions, AND there's
51	binder shadowing (`Pi.π (fun i ↦ ...)` rebinds `i`). The discrimination tree
52	cannot determine the `?G` placeholder.
53	
54	`simp only [Preadditive.sum_comp]`, `simp_rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]`,
55	`simp only [key₂]`, `simp_rw [key₂]` — all "no progress" (same HOU pre-filter
56	failure).
57	
58	### Attempt log (iter-094)
59	
60	#### Attempt 1: `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` + variants
61	- **Approach**: standard rfl-unfold of `(f ∘ₗ g) x`.
62	- **Result**: FAILED — "no progress" / "pattern `(?f ∘ₛₗ ?g) ?x` not found".
63	- **Diagnosis**: eqToHom's implicit codomain is Pi-product, def-equal but not
64	  syntactically equal to `∏ᶜ Z₂`. Pattern-tree rejects.
65	- **Dead-end**: confirmed iter-093's report.
66	
67	#### Attempt 2 (BREAKTHROUGH): `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]`
68	- **Approach**: fuse `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` into `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` at
69	  the categorical level via `ModuleCat.hom_comp`'s reverse direction.
70	- **Result**: SUCCESS. Goal now contains `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom z` (no `∘ₗ`).
71	  Diagnostics empty.
72	- **Key insight**: The reverse-direction rewrite trades a LinearMap-level
73	  eqToHom blocker for a categorical-level sum, which is a strictly easier shape.
74	
75	#### Attempt 3: `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]` (forward distribution)
76	- **Approach**: distribute `(∑F) ≫ E = ∑ i, F i ≫ E`.
77	- **Result**: FAILED — HOU pattern `(∑ j ∈ ?s, ?f j) ≫ ?g` not unified.
78	- **Diagnosis**: summand has nested `i` references + variable shadowing.
79	
80	#### Attempt 4: body-local `key₂` + `rw [key₂]`
81	- **Approach**: prove the distribution body-locally with free `G`, `E`, `z`
82	  (HOU-friendly), then apply via `rw`.
83	- **Result**: PROOF of `key₂` succeeds. `rw [key₂]` application FAILS (same HOU).
84	- **Lesson**: The HOU issue is at pattern-matching, not proof construction.
85	  Body-local proofs don't help if rewrite still has to do HOU unification.
86	
87	#### Attempt 5: `key₃` bundling outer `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom`
88	- **Approach**: bundle the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom (...)` with the distribution
89	  into a single helper.
90	- **Result**: FAILED — `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom` doesn't accept `(G i ≫ E).hom z`-typed
91	  input directly because the codomain is the Pi-product (def-equal but not
92	  syntactically equal to `∏ᶜ Z₂`). Same eqToHom obstruction at meta-level.
93	
94	#### Attempt 6: `simp` ensemble: `[Preadditive.sum_comp, ModuleCat.hom_sum, LinearMap.sum_apply, map_sum, Finset.smul_sum]`
95	- **Result**: "no progress" — HOU pre-filter rejects the first lemma; cascade
96	  doesn't fire.
97	
98	#### Attempt 7: `set F :=` with explicit summand
99	- **Result**: type elaboration failure on `(-1)^↑i` (heterogeneous power
100	  inference) and Pi.π type mismatch.
101	
102	### Concrete next step (for iter-095)
103	
104	**Route (D')**: Try `convert key₂ G_explicit E_explicit z`-style at the goal,
105	which generates equality subgoals that may be discharge-able by `rfl` or
106	`ext`. The `convert` tactic bypasses the discrimination tree's strict
107	unification.
108	
109	**Route (D'')**: Use `Finset.cons_induction` on `Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))`
110	to MANUALLY distribute the sum step-by-step (avoiding HOU entirely).
111	
112	**Route (D''')**: Reformulate the proof to push `Pi.π Z₂ j` through the
113	categorical sum BEFORE `.hom`-application, via
114	`Preadditive.comp_sum` (the dual of `Preadditive.sum_comp`). The
115	post-Preadditive.comp_sum form would be `∑ i, F i ≫ eqToHom ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j`, where
116	the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` is now INSIDE each summand's `≫`. This is the
117	most robust approach but requires careful setup.
118	
119	### Final `lean_diagnostic_messages` output (mandatory)
120	
121	```

123	```
124	**FILE COMPILES.** 0 errors. 6 sorries (L589, L681, L1005, L1033, L1223, L1252)
125	— unchanged from iter-093 entry state. No new axioms.

127	### Sorry budget tracking
128	
129	- Hard cap iter-094: 6.
130	- Actual iter-094: 6 (preserved).
131	- Substantive progress: +1 committed tactic line (`rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]`)
132	  AND +1 proved body-local helper (`key₂`), refining the blocker from

134	  fails on the distribution of `(∑F) ≫ E` for an `i`-shadowed/double-referencing
135	  summand".
136	
137	### Preserved iter-093 state (byte-for-byte)
138	
139	- L551–L555: `have hom_sum_dist` (iter-092 rebuild).
140	- L570–L577: `have key₁` (iter-093 proof).

143	
144	- Single-line `-- S6 step (b') [iter-094 BREAKTHROUGH]` annotating `← ModuleCat.hom_comp`.
145	- Brief comment block (8 lines) at `key₂` describing the HOU rewrite blocker for

147	
148	### Blueprint
149

151	entry in `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` — no blueprint edits this iter

k : Type u
inst✝ : Field k
C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))
U : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↑C.left.toTopCat
hU : IsAffineOpen U
s₀ : Finset ↑Γ(C.left, U)
n : ℕ
hn : 0 < n
R : CommRingCat := Γ(C.left, U)
K₀ : CochainComplex (ModuleCat k) ℕ := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover ↑s₀)
scK₀ : ShortComplex (ModuleCat k) := HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n
Z₁ : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ModuleCat k :=
  fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i a))))
Z₂ : (Fin (n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ModuleCat k :=
  fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i a))))
e₁ : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ≃ₗ[k] ↑(ModuleCat.of k ((i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₁ i))) :=
  (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv
e₂ : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) ≃ₗ[k] ↑(ModuleCat.of k ((i : Fin (n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₂ i))) := (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).toLinearEquiv
r : ↑R
y : (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₁ i)
perI₁ : (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → Module ↑R ↑(Z₁ i) :=
  fun i ↦ (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ⋯).op)).toModule
h_mod_pi₁ : Module (↑R) ((i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₁ i)) :=
  Pi.module (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) (fun i ↦ ↑(Z₁ i)) ↑R
perI₂ : (i : Fin (n + 1) → ↥s₀) → Module ↑R ↑(Z₂ i) :=
  fun i ↦ (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE ⋯).op)).toModule
h_mod_pi₂ : Module (↑R) ((i : Fin (n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₂ i)) := Pi.module (Fin (n + 1) → ↥s₀) (fun i ↦ ↑(Z₂ i)) ↑R
j : Fin (n + 1) → ↥s₀
hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n
hom_sum_dist :
  ∀ {M N : ModuleCat k} {ι : Type} (f : ι → (M ⟶ N)) (s : Finset ι),

393	/-- Iter-060 helper: bridge from `ExactAt n` on the Čech cochain complex to
394	`Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)`.
395	
396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an
397	`ExactAt n` hypothesis into `IsZero ((cechCochain ...).homology n)`, and
398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`
399	object to `Subsingleton` on its underlying carrier type.
400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	/-- Iter-087 (refactor): per-summand R-linear restriction-collapse. For nested
413	opens `V ≤ W ≤ U` in a scheme `C.left`, the chain of presheaf restrictions
414	`Γ(U) → Γ(W) → Γ(V)` collapses to the direct restriction `Γ(U) → Γ(V)`.
415	
416	Used in the proof of R-linearity of the Čech differential
417	(`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`): each per-summand restriction chain
418	`R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(C.left, V_j)` collapses to the
419	direct map `R → Γ(C.left, V_j)`, matching the per-i R-action induced by the
420	restriction algebra structure on the cochain factor.
421	
422	Extracted iter-087 from the inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` (iter-086) inside
423	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. The body is the
424	2-tactic proof verified by the iter-086 prover. -/
425	lemma presheafMap_restrict_collapse
426	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
427	    {V W U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
428	    (h_VW : V ≤ W) (h_VU : V ≤ U) (h_WU : W ≤ U)
429	    (r : C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U)) :
430	    (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VW).op).hom
431	        ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_WU).op).hom r) =
432	      (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VU).op).hom r := by
433	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
434	  congr 1
435	
436	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
437	/-- Iter-087 (refactor, CORRECTIVE): R-linearity of the Čech-cochain
438	differential at degree `(prev n, n)` in the product representation, specialized
439	to the project's Čech-cochain context (`basicOpenCover` of an affine `U` from a
440	spanning finset `s₀`).
441	
442	**Statement specialization.** The previous iter-087 abstraction quantified
443	universally over `(Z₁, Z₂, e₁, e₂, scK₀_f, h_mod_pi₁, h_mod_pi₂, R)`, yielding
444	a mathematically false statement for arbitrary `scK₀_f`. This corrective
445	version fixes `R`, `K₀`, `scK₀`, `Z_i`, `e_i`, `h_mod_pi_i` from the
446	Čech-cochain context, so the conclusion is mathematically true: the Čech
447	differential is a finite alternating sum of presheaf restriction maps, each
448	R-linear (see `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`), hence the sum is R-linear
449	componentwise.

115:  ConcreteCategory.hom (C := ModuleCat R) f
122:/-- Use the `ConcreteCategory.hom` projection for `@[simps]` lemmas. -/

110	
111	variable {R}
112	
113	/-- Turn a morphism in `ModuleCat` back into a `LinearMap`. -/
114	abbrev Hom.hom {A B : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : Hom A B) :=
115	  ConcreteCategory.hom (C := ModuleCat R) f
116	
117	/-- Typecheck a `LinearMap` as a morphism in `ModuleCat`. -/
118	abbrev ofHom {X Y : Type v} [AddCommGroup X] [Module R X] [AddCommGroup Y] [Module R Y]
119	    (f : X →ₗ[R] Y) : of R X ⟶ of R Y :=
120	  ConcreteCategory.ofHom (C := ModuleCat R) f
121	
122	/-- Use the `ConcreteCategory.hom` projection for `@[simps]` lemmas. -/
123	def Hom.Simps.hom (A B : ModuleCat.{v} R) (f : Hom A B) :=
124	  f.hom
125	
126	initialize_simps_projections Hom (hom' → hom)
127	
128	/-!
129	The results below duplicate the `ConcreteCategory` simp lemmas, but we can keep them for `dsimp`.
130	-/
131	
132	@[simp]
133	lemma hom_id {M : ModuleCat.{v} R} : (𝟙 M : M ⟶ M).hom = LinearMap.id := rfl
134	
135	/- Provided for rewriting. -/
136	lemma id_apply (M : ModuleCat.{v} R) (x : M) :
137	    (𝟙 M : M ⟶ M) x = x := by simp
138	
139	@[simp]

# Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` body L593 trailing sorry (iter-095)

### Iter-095 outcome summary

**Status**: NO substantive progress beyond iter-094. The HOU blocker on
the Čech alternating-sum body is structural (eqToHom-bridged Pi-product /
unfolded carrier type mismatch); ALL THREE planned routes (G), (H), (I)
and several variants are blocked. File compiles, 6 sorries (unchanged
from iter-094). Sorry budget hard cap (6) maintained.

### Single committed tactic (cosmetic cleanup at L592)

```lean
rw [show ModuleCat.Hom.hom = ConcreteCategory.hom (C := ModuleCat.{u} k) from rfl]
```

This converts `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` to `ConcreteCategory.hom` uniformly in
the goal, since they are abbrev-equal (`Hom.hom f := ConcreteCategory.hom
(C := ModuleCat R) f` per `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic`
L113-115). Cosmetic only — no structural advance. LSP-verified
diagnostics empty.

### Goal at trailing `sorry` (L593) — verbatim from `lean_goal`

```
(ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z₂ j))
      ((ConcreteCategory.hom
          ((∑ i, (-1) ^ ↑i •
              Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦
                Pi.π (fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(...basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i...))
                    (i_1 ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (SimplexCategory.δ i))) ≫
                  (toModuleKPresheaf C).map
                    (Pi.lift fun x ↦
                        Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1)
                          ((SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (SimplexCategory.δ i)) x)).op) ≫
            eqToHom ⋯))
        ((ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv.symm (r • y))) =
    r • (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z₂ j))
        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (... same expression ...))
         ((ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁).toLinearEquiv.symm y))
```

Both sides have outer `ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z₂ j)` applied to inner
`ConcreteCategory.hom ((∑F i) ≫ eqToHom ⋯)` applied to
`e₁.symm (r • y)` (LHS) / `e₁.symm y` (RHS).

### Route attempt log

#### Route (G) — Explicit-type `set F` (PLAN PRIMARY — FAILED)

**Tried**:
```lean
set F : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶
                       (∏ᶜ (fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(...)) : ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
  fun i => ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ)) • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ... (verbatim body) ...
```

**Outcome**: ELABORATION SUCCEEDS (no error). But the `set` doesn't fold
the goal — i.e., the literal sum body remains in the goal after `set`,
NOT replaced with `∑ i, F i`. **Diagnosis**: `set` only renames upon
EXACT syntactic match. The plan's recipe required `change` first to fold
the inner `Pi.π (fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(...))` back to a named `Z₁`-like
form, but the inner family uses `basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i` (composition
notation) whereas Z₁'s body uses `fun a ↦ basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (i a)`
(applied). These are eta-equivalent but not syntactically identical, so
the `change` fold cannot be expressed with `Z₁` directly.

The plan's recipe (`change` + `set F` + `rw [key₂ F]`) is in principle
sound but requires constructing a `change` target that EXACTLY matches
the goal's literal expression — which itself contains the same HOU
blocker we're trying to break. This is circular.

#### Route (H) — Second `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` to absorb outer `(Pi.π Z₂ j)` (FAILED)

**Tried**: several variants of `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` after
normalising the outer `Pi.π Z₂ j` to `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` form via
`show (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom _ = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom _` and via the cosmetic
rewrite of `ModuleCat.Hom.hom = ConcreteCategory.hom`.

**Outcome**: `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` FAILS at the outer because the
pattern is `g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom` (LinearMap composition), but our goal has
`g.hom (f.hom x)` (function application). The natural intermediate
`← LinearMap.comp_apply` (to convert `f (g x)` to `(f ∘ₗ g) x`) ALSO
fails — pattern `?f (?g ?x)` doesn't match because the intermediate
type between the outer `Pi.π Z₂ j` (input `∏ᶜ Z₂`) and the inner
`(∑F i) ≫ eqToHom` (output `ModuleCat.of k (∀ i, ↑(Z₂ i))`, the eqToHom
target) is def-equal but not syntactically equal — `LinearMap.comp_apply`
requires syntactic intermediate-type match.

**Tried `← CategoryTheory.comp_apply`** (the categorical-level analog
in `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.Basic`):
```lean
rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply]
```
The pattern is `(ConcreteCategory.hom ?g) ((ConcreteCategory.hom ?f) ?x)`.
The pattern doesn't unify with the goal because reconstructing the
categorical `?f ≫ ?g` requires `?f`'s codomain to syntactically equal
`?g`'s domain — and the eqToHom-bridged Pi-product / unfolded carrier
types are def-equal but not syntactically equal. **This is the
fundamental Route (H) blocker.**

#### Route (I) — `Finset.cons_induction` on `Finset.univ` (FAILED — typeclass synthesis stuck)

**Tried**:
```lean
induction Finset.univ using Finset.cons_induction with
| empty => simp
| cons i s hi ih => sorry
```

**Outcome**: FAILED with `typeclass instance problem is stuck: Fintype ?m.1151`
(also `AddCommMonoid ?m.1153` for `Finset.sum_induction`). Lean cannot
determine the carrier type for `Finset.univ` because the sum's
underlying carrier is implicit and the body has `i` in non-Miller
positions.

To make Route (I) work, the WHOLE goal would need to be rephrased
without the implicit `Finset.univ`, e.g., by generalising to an
arbitrary `s : Finset (Fin (n+1))` outside the goal. But this requires
abstracting the sum body — which itself has the HOU blocker we're
trying to break.

#### Route (X) — `Preadditive.sum_comp` variants via `conv_lhs => arg N; rw [...]` (FAILED)

**Tried**: `conv_lhs => arg 1; arg 1; rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]`,
`conv => rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]`, etc.

**Outcome**: FAILED — `conv` navigates correctly but at each focus point,
the pattern `(∑ j ∈ ?s, ?f j) ≫ ?g` doesn't match because the summand
body has `i` in non-Miller positions (HOU pre-filter rejection),
identical to the direct `rw` failure mode.

#### Route (Y) — direct `simp only [Pi.lift_π_apply]` and similar (FAILED)

**Tried**: `simp only [Pi.lift_π_apply]`, `simp only [Pi.lift_π]`,
ensemble simps.

**Outcome**: ALL "no progress" — the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ j)` and the inner
`Pi.lift` are separated by the eqToHom and the categorical composition,
so `Pi.lift_π` cannot fire (it needs `Pi.lift ≫ Pi.π = ...` shape,
not `Pi.π (Pi.lift ≫ ... ≫ eqToHom)` shape).

### Concrete next step (for iter-096)

The HOU blocker is fundamentally STRUCTURAL: the goal's summand body
references `i` in nested positions (`(-1)^↑i`, `SimplexCategory.δ i`
appearing TWICE: in the inner `Pi.π` and in the outer `.op`-wrapped
`Pi.lift`), making the summand NOT Miller-abstractable. Combined with
the eqToHom bridge between `∏ᶜ Z₂` and the unfolded
`ModuleCat.of k (∀ i, ↑(Z₂ i))` carrier, no `rw`-style or `simp`-style
distribution can fire.

**Recommended iter-096 escalation**: refactor subagent. Concretely:

1. **Bypass `Pi.lift`-of-`Pi.π`-composition.** Reformulate `scK₀.f`
   coordinate-wise from the start: define
   `scK₀.f.hom : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[k] (∀ j, Z₂ j)`
   directly as a sum `(s • y) j := Σᵢ (-1)^i (presheaf-restriction)(y (j ∘ δᵢ))`
   in coordinates, BYPASSING the categorical Pi.lift / eqToHom
   construction entirely. R-linearity then follows from per-summand
   R-linearity of each presheaf-restriction.

2. **Strategy for the refactor**: extract `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` to a
   reformulation that quantifies over a **fresh, abstract**
   `F : Fin (n+1) → ((∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[k] (∀ j, Z₂ j))` family with
   per-`i` R-linearity hypotheses, then prove the alternating sum's
   R-linearity from those hypotheses. The actual Čech sum can then be
   plugged in at the call site, after the alternating-sum lemma is
   factored out.

3. **Why this works**: the abstract version `F : Fin(n+1) → R-linear-map`
   has no nested `i` references in non-Miller positions (the `i` is
   only the binder for `F`), so the structural lemma's proof goes
   through with standard HOU. The plug-in at the call site then closes
   the goal modulo a coordinate-wise R-linearity hypothesis per `Fᵢ`,
   which the `presheaf-restriction`-R-linearity supplies directly.

This requires REFACTORING the signature of `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` (or
adding a new top-level helper). It is a 1-2 iteration refactor; the
mathematical content is unchanged.

### Sorry budget tracking (iter-095)

- Hard cap: 6.
- Actual iter-095: 6 (preserved; no regression).
- Substantive progress: 0 new committed tactic lines beyond iter-094;
  cosmetic `ModuleCat.Hom.hom → ConcreteCategory.hom` normalisation only.
- Iter-094 BREAKTHROUGH preserved: `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` at L570,
